---
title: "Energy Commodities Report"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 6
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight: kate
    self_contained: true
    css: "/Users/benjaminlachavanne/Desktop/script/finance_analysis/html/style/style.css"


params:
  llm_analysis: false
  
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

# Main path 
path_main <- file.path("~", "Desktop", "script", "finance_analysis")

# Upload packages, functions and inputs 
source(paste0(path_main,"/initialization/package.R"))         # |Package|
source(paste0(path_main,"/function/function_information.R"))  # |Function price|
source(paste0(path_main,"/function/function_format.R"))       # |Function format|
source(paste0(path_main,"/function/function_visualization.R"))# |Functions visualization|
source(paste0(path_main,"/function/function_compute.R"))      # |Functions indicator|
source(paste0(path_main,"/function/function_ai.R"))           # |Functions ai|
source(paste0(path_main,"/initialization/input.R"))           # |Input|

# Download all series from the RDS folder
path_rds_folder    <- "/Users/benjaminlachavanne/Desktop/script/finance_analysis/database/rds/"
path_rds_all <- paste0(path_rds_folder,"series_store_all.rds")
series_database <- readRDS(path_rds_all)

# Styled info box
series_info_box <- function(source_html, frequency, adjustment, about) {
  htmltools::HTML(paste0('
  <div class="info-card">
    <div class="info-grid">
      <div class="info-row">
        <span class="info-label">Source</span>
        <span class="info-value">', source_html, '</span>
      </div>
      <div class="info-row">
        <span class="info-label">Frequency</span>
        <span class="info-value">', frequency, '</span>
      </div>
      <div class="info-row">
        <span class="info-label">Adjustment</span>
        <span class="info-value">', adjustment, '</span>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="info-about">', about, '</div>
  </div>
  '))
}

# Section label helper
section_label <- function(icon, text) {
  htmltools::HTML(paste0(
    '<div class="section-label"><span class="section-icon">', icon,
    '</span><span class="section-text">', text, '</span></div>'))
}

# Subsection label helper
subsection_label <- function(text) {
  htmltools::HTML(paste0('<div class="subsection-label">', text, '</div>'))
}

# Chart note helper
chart_note <- function(text) {
  htmltools::HTML(paste0('<p class="chart-note">', text, '</p>'))
}
```


<!-- ####################################################################### -->
<!-- Head Title  -->
<!-- ####################################################################### -->

```{=html}
<!-- ── MASTHEAD ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<div class="masthead">
  <div class="masthead-eyebrow">Commodities Research · Global Markets</div>
  <div class="masthead-title">Commodities <span>Report</span></div>
  <div class="masthead-date">Latest update date: `r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`</div>
</div>
```

<!-- ####################################################################### -->
<!-- Abstract  -->
<!-- ####################################################################### -->

## Abstract

<!-- ####################################################################### -->
<!-- Prices  -->
<!-- ####################################################################### -->

## Prices

### Table

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r COMMODITIES TABLE, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Mapping tickers to readable names
labels <- c(
  "BZ=F"    = "Brent Crude Oil",
  "CL=F"    = "WTI Crude Oil",
  "HO=F"    = "Heating Oil",
  "RB=F"    = "RBOB Gasoline",
  "NG=F"    = "Natural Gas",
  "TTF=F"   = "TTF Natural Gas"
)

# Get series
commodities <- c("BZ=F","CL=F","NG=F","HO=F","RB=F", "TTF=F")

# Plot table
div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
    produce_performance_table(series = commodities, label = labels))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### Oil Prices

#### *Brent Crude Oil*

```{r ENERGY BRENT TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Yahoo — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/BZ=F/" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "Brent Crude is the international oil benchmark, sourced from the North Sea and priced in USD per barrel. It serves as the reference price for roughly two-thirds of global crude oil trading. Sensitive to OPEC+ supply decisions, geopolitical tensions, and global demand conditions. Quoted in USD per barrel, updated daily with closing prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r ENERGY BRENT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("fut_brent_crude"))

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *WTI Crude Oil*

```{r ENERGY WTI TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Yahoo — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CL=F/" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "WTI (West Texas Intermediate) is the primary US oil benchmark, produced in the Permian Basin and delivered at Cushing, Oklahoma. It trades at a spread to Brent, influenced by US production dynamics, pipeline infrastructure, and domestic inventory levels. Sensitive to Fed policy, the US dollar, and shale production costs. Quoted in USD per barrel, updated daily with closing prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r ENERGY WTI, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("fut_wti_crude"))

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *Heating Oil*

```{r ENERGY HO TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Yahoo — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/HO=F/" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "Heating Oil futures track No. 2 distillate fuel, a key refined petroleum product used for residential heating and as a diesel fuel proxy. Listed on NYMEX, it is closely correlated with crude oil prices and refinery margins. Demand is highly seasonal, peaking in Northern Hemisphere winters. Quoted in USD per gallon, updated daily with closing prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r ENERGY HO, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("fut_heating_oil"))

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *RBOB Gasoline*

```{r ENERGY RBOB TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Yahoo — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/RB=F/" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "RBOB Gasoline futures represent the US benchmark for unleaded gasoline, listed on NYMEX. Prices are driven by crude oil input costs, refinery utilization, seasonal demand (summer driving season), and US inventory data. Closely tracked as an indicator of downstream energy consumer conditions. Quoted in USD per gallon, updated daily with closing prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r ENERGY RBOB, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("fut_rbob_gasoline"))

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### Gas Prices

#### *Natural Gas*

```{r ENERGY NATGAS TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Yahoo — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NG=F/" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "The Henry Hub Natural Gas futures contract is the US benchmark for natural gas, priced at the Henry Hub pipeline interconnection in Louisiana. Highly volatile and seasonally driven, it is sensitive to weather conditions, storage levels, LNG export demand, and power generation needs. Quoted in USD per MMBtu, updated daily with closing prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r ENERGY NATGAS, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("fut_natural_gas"))

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *TTF Natural Gas*

```{r ENERGY NATGAS TTF TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Yahoo — <a href="https://fr.finance.yahoo.com/quote/TTF%3DF/" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "TTF Natural Gas Futures measures the price of natural gas for future delivery at the Title Transfer Facility virtual trading hub in the Netherlands, the primary European benchmark for natural gas pricing. TTF serves as the reference price for the majority of European natural gas contracts and is sensitive to European storage levels, Russian pipeline supply dynamics, global LNG import competition, seasonal heating and cooling demand, and renewable energy output fluctuations that affect gas-fired power generation demand. Quoted in euros per megawatt-hour, updated daily with closing prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r ENERGY NATGAS TTF, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("fut_ttf_natural_gas"))

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### Comparison

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r COMMO COMP ENERGY, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("fut_brent_crude", "fut_wti_crude", "fut_heating_oil", "fut_rbob_gasoline", "fut_natural_gas","fut_ttf_natural_gas"))

# Compute index
data <- compute_rebase(df = data, base = "2015-01-07")

# Plot series
div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
    produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE, title = "Energy Futures – Indexed Performance (2015:01:07 = 100)"))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```


<!-- ####################################################################### -->
<!-- Indicators  -->
<!-- ####################################################################### -->

## Indicators

### Oil Indicators

#### *US Crude Oil Inventory*

```{r INV CRUDE TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Investing.com — <a href="https://www.investing.com/economic-calendar/eia-crude-oil-inventories-75" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Weekly",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "EIA Crude Oil Inventories measures the weekly change in U.S. commercial crude oil stocks as reported by the U.S. Energy Information Administration from its Weekly Petroleum Status Report, covering crude oil held at refineries, tank farms, and pipelines across the United States, explicitly excluding volumes held in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve. Measured in thousands of barrels, the report also provides a breakdown of stocks at Cushing, Oklahoma, the designated physical delivery point for WTI futures contracts traded on NYMEX, making the Cushing sub-component particularly closely watched by energy traders as the most direct fundamental input into WTI price formation. A larger-than-expected build in inventories signals oversupply conditions or weakening refinery demand and typically exerts downward pressure on WTI prices, while a larger-than-expected draw indicates tightening supply conditions and supports higher prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r INV CRUDE, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("us_crude_inv"))

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE)))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *Crude Oil Production*

```{r PRO CRUDE TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'EIA — <a href="https://www.eia.gov/opendata/browser/international?frequency=monthly&data=value;&sortColumn=period;&sortDirection=desc;" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Monthly",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "Crude Oil Production measures monthly crude oil output including lease condensate for major producing countries and regional aggregates, reported by the US Energy Information Administration from its International Energy Statistics database. Expressed in thousands of barrels per day, the dataset covers both the OPEC aggregate and its individual member countries including Saudi Arabia, Iraq, the UAE, Kuwait, Iran, Venezuela, Libya, and Nigeria as well as major non-OPEC producers such as the United States, Russia, Canada, and Brazil. OPEC production decisions, coordinated through ministerial meetings and formalized in output quota agreements, represent the single most important supply-side lever in global oil markets and directly influence Brent and WTI price formation; non-OPEC producers particularly the US through shale output act as the primary swing factor determining the residual supply balance. Rising production from any major producer typically pressures prices lower by adding to global supply, while coordinated OPEC cuts, geopolitical disruptions, or unexpected field outages tighten the supply balance and support higher prices. Not seasonally adjusted, released monthly.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r PRO CRUDE, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data_all <- get_series(c("opec_crude_prod", "us_crude_prod", "ru_crude_prod", "cn_crude_prod",
                          "sau_crude_prod",  "irq_crude_prod", "irn_crude_prod", "are_crude_prod",
                          "kwt_crude_prod",  "ven_crude_prod", "nga_crude_prod", "lby_crude_prod",
                          "dza_crude_prod",  "nor_crude_prod", "can_crude_prod", "mex_crude_prod",
                          "bra_crude_prod",  "kaz_crude_prod"))

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data_all, sma = FALSE, title = "Crude Oil Production by Country (kb/d)")),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : OPEC = OPEC aggregate &nbsp;|&nbsp; USA = United States &nbsp;|&nbsp; RUS = Russia &nbsp;|&nbsp; CHN = China &nbsp;|&nbsp; SAU = Saudi Arabia &nbsp;|&nbsp; IRQ = Iraq &nbsp;|&nbsp; IRN = Iran &nbsp;|&nbsp; ARE = United Arab Emirates &nbsp;|&nbsp; KWT = Kuwait &nbsp;|&nbsp; VEN = Venezuela &nbsp;|&nbsp; NGA = Nigeria &nbsp;|&nbsp; LBY = Libya &nbsp;|&nbsp; DZA = Algeria &nbsp;|&nbsp; NOR = Norway &nbsp;|&nbsp; CAN = Canada &nbsp;|&nbsp; MEX = Mexico &nbsp;|&nbsp; BRA = Brazil &nbsp;|&nbsp; KAZ = Kazakhstan</em></small>")))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *Rig Count Baker Hughes*

```{r RIG TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Investing.com — <a href="https://www.investing.com/economic-calendar/baker-hughes-u.s.-rig-count-1652" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Weekly",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "Baker Hughes US Rig Count measures the number of active drilling rigs exploring for or developing oil and natural gas across the United States, published weekly every Friday by Baker Hughes, a leading oilfield services company. The count covers both oil-directed and gas-directed rigs operating onshore and offshore, with the oil rig count receiving particular attention as a forward-looking proxy for future US crude production, given that each new well drilled today translates into incremental supply over the following months. Because drilling activity responds rapidly to changes in commodity prices and producer economics, the rig count serves as one of the most timely leading indicators of US shale output and domestic supply trajectory, historically exhibiting a lagged correlation of three to six months with WTI prices. A rising rig count signals that producers are expanding drilling programs in anticipation of sustained or higher prices, pointing to future supply growth that could pressure prices; a declining count reflects producer capital discipline or margin compression, signaling forthcoming supply contraction that tends to support prices. The US rig count is particularly significant in the context of global oil markets given the dominant role of American shale production as the primary non-OPEC swing supply factor.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r RIG, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("us_rig_count"))

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE)))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *Crack Spread*

```{r CRA TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'RBN Energy — <a href="https://rbnenergy.com/market-data/3-2-1-crack-spread" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "3-2-1 Crack Spread measures the gross refining margin earned by processing three barrels of WTI crude oil into two barrels of RBOB gasoline and one barrel of heating oil, serving as the most widely used proxy for US refinery profitability. Expressed in US dollars per barrel, it is calculated as the weighted value of the two refined products minus the cost of the three barrels of crude input, with the 3-2-1 ratio approximating the typical output yield of a US refinery. A widening crack spread signals strong demand for refined products relative to crude, incentivizing refineries to increase throughput and ultimately driving higher crude oil demand upstream; a narrowing spread indicates compressed margins and leads refineries to reduce processing runs, softening crude demand. Because refinery margins respond to real-time shifts in product demand, seasonal consumption patterns, refinery capacity utilization, and crude quality differentials, the crack spread serves as a leading indicator of physical crude oil demand and a key tool for understanding price transmission across the full oil value chain from wellhead to pump. Updated daily with closing prices of the three components.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r CRA, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series and compute crack spread
data <- get_series(c("fut_rbob_gasoline", "fut_heating_oil", "fut_wti_crude")) %>%
  select(date, value, name) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = name, values_from = value) %>%
  mutate(value = ((2 * fut_rbob_gasoline * 42) + (1 * fut_heating_oil * 42) - (3 * fut_wti_crude)) / 3, name = "crack_spread", id = "CRASPR") %>%
  select(date, value, name, id)

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE, title = "3-2-1 Crack Spread (USD/barrel)")),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>
        Crack Spread &nbsp;=&nbsp; 
        <span style='border-top: 1px solid black; display: inline-block; padding-top: 2px;'>
          &nbsp;( 2 &times; RBOB &times; 42 )&nbsp;+&nbsp;( HO &times; 42 )&nbsp;&minus;&nbsp;( 3 &times; WTI )&nbsp;
        </span>
        &nbsp;&divide;&nbsp; 3
        &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp;
        RBOB &amp; HO in USD/gal &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp; WTI in USD/bbl &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp; &times;42 converts gal &rarr; bbl
      </em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### Gaz Indicators

#### *US Natural Gas Storage*

```{r US GAZ STO TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Investing.com — <a href="https://www.investing.com/economic-calendar/natural-gas-storage-386" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Weekly",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "EIA Natural Gas Storage measures the weekly change in the volume of natural gas held in underground storage facilities across the United States, reported by the US Energy Information Administration from its Weekly Natural Gas Storage Report. Expressed in billions of cubic feet, the report covers working gas in storage across three regions (East, Midwest, and South Central) as well as mountain and Pacific regions, and is released every Thursday for the prior week. A larger than expected build in inventories signals weaker than anticipated demand or excess supply and is bearish for natural gas prices; a larger than expected draw indicates stronger demand or tighter supply and supports higher prices. Storage levels are particularly closely watched relative to the five year historical average for the corresponding week of the year, as significant deviations from seasonal norms provide the clearest signal of supply and demand imbalance in the natural gas market. Because natural gas demand is highly seasonal, driven by winter heating and summer power generation needs, storage dynamics are a primary driver of Henry Hub price volatility especially during periods of extreme weather or supply disruptions.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r US GAZ STO, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("us_rig_count"))

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE)))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```



<!-- ####################################################################### -->
<!-- Indices  -->
<!-- ####################################################################### -->

## Indices

### Broad Commodities

#### *S&P GSCI*

```{r IDX SPGSCI TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Yahoo — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5ESPGSCI/" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "The S&P GSCI is a broad, production-weighted commodity index maintained by S&P Dow Jones Indices, covering energy, metals, and agriculture. Its heavy energy weighting (typically 50–70%) means it closely tracks oil market dynamics. Widely used as a benchmark for broad commodity exposure and as an inflation sensitivity gauge. Quoted in index points, updated daily with closing prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r IDX SPGSCI, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("idx_spgsci"))

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *Bloomberg Commodity Index*

```{r IDX BCOM TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Investing.com — <a href="https://www.investing.com/indices/bloomberg-commodity" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "The Bloomberg Commodity Index tracks a diversified basket of commodity futures across energy, metals, and agriculture, with sector caps limiting any single group to 33% and any single commodity to 15%. Its more balanced weighting relative to the S&P GSCI makes it a preferred benchmark for diversified commodity exposure without undue energy concentration. Quoted in index points, updated daily with closing prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r IDX BCOM, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(c("idx_bcom"))

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot series
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```
